title: thymus serpyllum var. carniolicus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. carniolicus, also known as the Alpine Thyme or Carniolan Thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus serpyllum plant, a low-growing, herbaceous perennial native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum var. carniolicus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antiviral and antifungal properties. It can be consumed as a tea, used in essential oil form, or incorporated into ointments and salves, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
